revision.text:
'''山陵志'''（さんりょうし）は、[[江戸時代]]後期に[[蒲生君平]]が著した書物で、天皇陵（山陵）に関する研究調査結果を記した[[史料]]である。
==概要== [[蒲生君平]]が[[寛政]]8年（[[1796年]]）から12年（[[1800年]]）にかけて自ら行った[[近畿地方]]や[[四国地方]]の古墳調査結果を編纂したもので、草稿は寛政9年（[[1797年]]）に、最終稿は寛政12年（[[1801年]]）に完成したと言われる。書物として発刊されたのは[[文化]]5年（[[1808年]]）とも[[文政]]5年（[[1822年]]）とも言われる。文政5年説の場合、君平の死後にはじめて出版されたことになる。「[[前方後円墳]]」という言葉は山陵志の中で初めて君平が用いた言葉である。本書が幕末の[[尊王論]]の根拠となったと評される一方で、調査結果を記録した[[考古学]]資料としての評価も高く、[[明治維新]]の後、[[明治天皇]]は君平の業績を讃え郷里である[[宇都宮市|宇都宮]]に石碑建立（勅旌碑）と[[蒲生神社]]創建する勅命を発することとなった。本書は[[水戸光圀]]が編纂に取り組んだ『[[大日本史]]』の補完資料の位置づけとしても評価されている。
==構成== 全2巻。 * 第1巻 ** [[大和国]]の山陵（31箇所） *** [[神武天皇]]陵 *** [[綏靖天皇]]陵 *** [[安寧天皇]]陵 *** [[懿德天皇]]陵 *** [[宣化天皇]]陵 *** [[孝元天皇]]陵 *** [[天武天皇]]および[[持統天皇]]陵（合葬） *** [[欽明天皇]]陵 *** [[文武天皇]]陵 *** [[齊明天皇]]陵 *** [[孝昭天皇]]陵 *** [[孝安天皇]]陵 *** [[後醍醐天皇]]陵 *** [[崇峻天皇]]陵 *** [[舒明天皇]]陵 *** [[崇神天皇]]陵 *** [[景行天皇]]陵 *** [[光仁天皇]]陵 *** [[開化天皇]]陵 *** [[聖武天皇]]陵 *** [[元明天皇]]陵 *** [[元正天皇]]陵 *** [[平城天皇]]陵 *** [[垂仁天皇]]陵 *** [[安康天皇]]陵 *** [[成務天皇]]陵 *** [[孝謙天皇]]陵 *** [[孝靈天皇]]陵 *** [[顯宗天皇]]陵 *** [[武烈天皇]]陵 ** [[河内国]]の山陵（13箇所） *** [[仲哀天皇]]陵 *** [[允恭天皇]]陵 *** [[應神天皇]]陵 *** [[雄略天皇]]陵 *** [[仁賢天皇]]陵 *** [[清寧天皇]]陵 *** [[安閑天皇]]陵 *** [[敏達天皇]]陵 *** [[用明天皇]]陵 *** [[推古天皇]]陵 *** [[孝徳天皇]]陵 *** [[後村上天皇]]陵 ** [[和泉国]]の山陵（3箇所） *** [[仁徳天皇]]陵 *** [[履中天皇]]陵 *** [[反正天皇]]陵 ** [[摂津国]]、[[丹波国]]、[[阿波国]]、[[淡路国]]、[[讃岐国]]、[[隠岐国]]、[[佐渡国]]（各1箇所、計7箇所） *** [[繼體天皇]]陵（摂津国） *** [[光嚴天皇]]陵（丹波国） *** [[土御門天皇]]陵（阿波国） *** [[淳仁天皇]]陵（淡路国）- 廢帝、降格され淡路公と号し配流先の淡路国で没した *** [[崇徳天皇]]陵（讃岐国） *** [[後鳥羽天皇]]陵（隠岐国） *** [[順徳天皇]]陵（佐渡国） * 第2巻 ** [[山城国]]（30箇所） *** [[天智天皇]]陵 *** [[醍醐天皇]]陵 *** [[朱雀天皇]]陵 *** [[桓武天皇]]陵 *** [[仁明天皇]]陵 *** [[後深草天皇]]陵 *** [[伏見天皇]]陵 *** [[後伏見天皇]]陵 *** [[後柏原天皇]]陵 *** [[後奈良天皇]]陵 *** [[白河天皇]]陵 *** [[淳和天皇]]陵 *** [[文徳天皇]]陵 *** [[光孝天皇]]陵 *** [[宇多天皇]]陵 *** [[後朱雀天皇]]陵 *** [[後冷泉天皇]]陵 *** [[村上天皇]]陵 *** [[圓融天皇]]陵 *** [[後村上天皇]]陵 *** [[一條天皇]]陵 *** [[堀河天皇]]陵 *** [[嵯峨天皇]]陵 *** [[龜山天皇]]陵 *** [[後龜山天皇]]陵 *** [[清和天皇]]陵 *** [[後三條天皇]]陵 *** [[二條天皇]]陵 *** [[華山天皇]]陵 *** [[三條天皇]]陵 *** [[後花園天皇]]陵 *** [[陽成天皇]]陵 *** [[後一條天皇]]陵 *** [[冷泉天皇]]陵 *** [[後一條天皇]]陵 *** [[高倉天皇]]陵 *** [[六條天皇]]陵 *** [[後白河天皇]]陵 *** [[後堀河天皇]]陵 *** [[四條天皇]]陵 *** [[後圓融天皇]]陵 *** [[後小松天皇]]陵 *** [[稱光天皇]]陵 *** [[後土御門天皇]]陵 *** [[稱光天皇]]陵 *** [[後土御門天皇]]陵 *** [[正親町天皇]]陵 *** [[後陽成天皇]]以降の陵
{{DEFAULTSORT:さんりようし}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:日本の歴史書]] [[Category:栃木県の歴史]]

'''山陵志'''（さんりょうし）は、[[江戸時代]]後期に[[蒲生君平]]が著した書物で、天皇陵（山陵）に関する研究調査結果を記した[[史料]]である。
==概要== [[蒲生君平]]が[[寛政]]8年（[[1796年]]）から12年（[[1800年]]）にかけて自ら行った[[近畿地方]]や[[四国地方]]の古墳調査結果を編纂したもので、草稿は寛政9年（[[1797年]]）に、最終稿は寛政12年（[[1801年]]）に完成したと言われる。書物として発刊されたのは[[文化 (日本)|文化]]5年（[[1808年]]）とも[[文政]]5年（[[1822年]]）とも言われる。文政5年説の場合、君平の死後にはじめて出版されたことになる。「[[前方後円墳]]」という言葉は山陵志の中で初めて君平が用いた言葉である。本書が幕末の[[尊王論]]の根拠となったと評される一方で、調査結果を記録した[[考古学]]資料としての評価も高く、[[明治維新]]の後、[[明治天皇]]は君平の業績を讃え郷里である[[宇都宮市|宇都宮]]に石碑建立（勅旌碑）と[[蒲生神社]]創建する勅命を発することとなった。本書は[[水戸光圀]]が編纂に取り組んだ『[[大日本史]]』の補完資料の位置づけとしても評価されている。
==構成== 全2巻。 * 第1巻 ** [[大和国]]の山陵（31箇所） *** [[神武天皇]]陵 *** [[綏靖天皇]]陵 *** [[安寧天皇]]陵 *** [[懿德天皇]]陵 *** [[宣化天皇]]陵 *** [[孝元天皇]]陵 *** [[天武天皇]]および[[持統天皇]]陵（合葬） *** [[欽明天皇]]陵 *** [[文武天皇]]陵 *** [[齊明天皇]]陵 *** [[孝昭天皇]]陵 *** [[孝安天皇]]陵 *** [[後醍醐天皇]]陵 *** [[崇峻天皇]]陵 *** [[舒明天皇]]陵 *** [[崇神天皇]]陵 *** [[景行天皇]]陵 *** [[光仁天皇]]陵 *** [[開化天皇]]陵 *** [[聖武天皇]]陵 *** [[元明天皇]]陵 *** [[元正天皇]]陵 *** [[平城天皇]]陵 *** [[垂仁天皇]]陵 *** [[安康天皇]]陵 *** [[成務天皇]]陵 *** [[孝謙天皇]]陵 *** [[孝靈天皇]]陵 *** [[顯宗天皇]]陵 *** [[武烈天皇]]陵 ** [[河内国]]の山陵（13箇所） *** [[仲哀天皇]]陵 *** [[允恭天皇]]陵 *** [[應神天皇]]陵 *** [[雄略天皇]]陵 *** [[仁賢天皇]]陵 *** [[清寧天皇]]陵 *** [[安閑天皇]]陵 *** [[敏達天皇]]陵 *** [[用明天皇]]陵 *** [[推古天皇]]陵 *** [[孝徳天皇]]陵 *** [[後村上天皇]]陵 ** [[和泉国]]の山陵（3箇所） *** [[仁徳天皇]]陵 *** [[履中天皇]]陵 *** [[反正天皇]]陵 ** [[摂津国]]、[[丹波国]]、[[阿波国]]、[[淡路国]]、[[讃岐国]]、[[隠岐国]]、[[佐渡国]]（各1箇所、計7箇所） *** [[繼體天皇]]陵（摂津国） *** [[光嚴天皇]]陵（丹波国） *** [[土御門天皇]]陵（阿波国） *** [[淳仁天皇]]陵（淡路国）- 廢帝、降格され淡路公と号し配流先の淡路国で没した *** [[崇徳天皇]]陵（讃岐国） *** [[後鳥羽天皇]]陵（隠岐国） *** [[順徳天皇]]陵（佐渡国） * 第2巻 ** [[山城国]]（30箇所） *** [[天智天皇]]陵 *** [[醍醐天皇]]陵 *** [[朱雀天皇]]陵 *** [[桓武天皇]]陵 *** [[仁明天皇]]陵 *** [[後深草天皇]]陵 *** [[伏見天皇]]陵 *** [[後伏見天皇]]陵 *** [[後柏原天皇]]陵 *** [[後奈良天皇]]陵 *** [[白河天皇]]陵 *** [[淳和天皇]]陵 *** [[文徳天皇]]陵 *** [[光孝天皇]]陵 *** [[宇多天皇]]陵 *** [[後朱雀天皇]]陵 *** [[後冷泉天皇]]陵 *** [[村上天皇]]陵 *** [[圓融天皇]]陵 *** [[後村上天皇]]陵 *** [[一條天皇]]陵 *** [[堀河天皇]]陵 *** [[嵯峨天皇]]陵 *** [[龜山天皇]]陵 *** [[後龜山天皇]]陵 *** [[清和天皇]]陵 *** [[後三條天皇]]陵 *** [[二條天皇]]陵 *** [[華山天皇]]陵 *** [[三條天皇]]陵 *** [[後花園天皇]]陵 *** [[陽成天皇]]陵 *** [[後一條天皇]]陵 *** [[冷泉天皇]]陵 *** [[後一條天皇]]陵 *** [[高倉天皇]]陵 *** [[六條天皇]]陵 *** [[後白河天皇]]陵 *** [[後堀河天皇]]陵 *** [[四條天皇]]陵 *** [[後圓融天皇]]陵 *** [[後小松天皇]]陵 *** [[稱光天皇]]陵 *** [[後土御門天皇]]陵 *** [[稱光天皇]]陵 *** [[後土御門天皇]]陵 *** [[正親町天皇]]陵 *** [[後陽成天皇]]以降の陵

'''山陵志'''（さんりょうし）は、[[江戸時代]]後期に[[蒲生君平]]が著した書物で、天皇陵（山陵）に関する研究調査結果を記した[[史料]]である。
==概要== [[蒲生君平]]が[[寛政]]8年（[[1796年]]）から12年（[[1800年]]）にかけて自ら行った[[近畿地方]]や[[四国地方]]の古墳調査結果を編纂したもので、草稿は寛政9年（[[1797年]]）に、最終稿は寛政12年（[[1801年]]）に完成したと言われる。書物として発刊されたのは[[文化 (日本)|文化]]5年（[[1808年]]）とも[[文政]]5年（[[1822年]]）とも言われる。文政5年説の場合、君平の死後にはじめて出版されたことになる。「[[前方後円墳]]」という言葉は山陵志の中で初めて君平が用いた言葉である。本書が幕末の[[尊王論]]の根拠となったと評される一方で、調査結果を記録した[[考古学]]資料としての評価も高く、[[明治維新]]の後、[[明治天皇]]は君平の業績を讃え郷里である[[宇都宮市|宇都宮]]に石碑建立（勅旌碑）と[[蒲生神社]]創建する勅命を発することとなった。本書は[[水戸光圀]]が編纂に取り組んだ『[[大日本史]]』の補完資料の位置づけとしても評価されている。
==構成== 全2巻。 * 第1巻 ** [[大和国]]の山陵（31箇所） *** [[神武天皇]]陵 *** [[綏靖天皇]]陵 *** [[安寧天皇]]陵 *** [[懿德天皇]]陵 *** [[宣化天皇]]陵 *** [[孝元天皇]]陵 *** [[天武天皇]]および[[持統天皇]]陵（合葬） *** [[欽明天皇]]陵 *** [[文武天皇]]陵 *** [[皇極天皇|齊明天皇]]陵 *** [[孝昭天皇]]陵 *** [[孝安天皇]]陵 *** [[後醍醐天皇]]陵 *** [[崇峻天皇]]陵 *** [[舒明天皇]]陵 *** [[崇神天皇]]陵 *** [[景行天皇]]陵 *** [[光仁天皇]]陵 *** [[開化天皇]]陵 *** [[聖武天皇]]陵 *** [[元明天皇]]陵 *** [[元正天皇]]陵 *** [[平城天皇]]陵 *** [[垂仁天皇]]陵 *** [[安康天皇]]陵 *** [[成務天皇]]陵 *** [[孝謙天皇]]陵 *** [[孝霊天皇|孝靈天皇]]陵 *** [[顕宗天皇|顯宗天皇]]陵 *** [[武烈天皇]]陵 ** [[河内国]]の山陵（13箇所） *** [[仲哀天皇]]陵 *** [[允恭天皇]]陵 *** [[応神天皇|應神天皇]]陵 *** [[雄略天皇]]陵 *** [[仁賢天皇]]陵 *** [[清寧天皇]]陵 *** [[安閑天皇]]陵 *** [[敏達天皇]]陵 *** [[用明天皇]]陵 *** [[推古天皇]]陵 *** [[孝徳天皇]]陵 *** [[後村上天皇]]陵 ** [[和泉国]]の山陵（3箇所） *** [[仁徳天皇]]陵 *** [[履中天皇]]陵 *** [[反正天皇]]陵 ** [[摂津国]]、[[丹波国]]、[[阿波国]]、[[淡路国]]、[[讃岐国]]、[[隠岐国]]、[[佐渡国]]（各1箇所、計7箇所） *** [[継体天皇|繼體天皇]]陵（摂津国） *** [[光厳天皇|光嚴天皇]]陵（丹波国） *** [[土御門天皇]]陵（阿波国） *** [[淳仁天皇]]陵（淡路国）- 廢帝、降格され淡路公と号し配流先の淡路国で没した *** [[崇徳天皇]]陵（讃岐国） *** [[後鳥羽天皇]]陵（隠岐国） *** [[順徳天皇]]陵（佐渡国） * 第2巻 ** [[山城国]]（30箇所） *** [[天智天皇]]陵 *** [[醍醐天皇]]陵 *** [[朱雀天皇]]陵 *** [[桓武天皇]]陵 *** [[仁明天皇]]陵 *** [[後深草天皇]]陵 *** [[伏見天皇]]陵 *** [[後伏見天皇]]陵 *** [[後柏原天皇]]陵 *** [[後奈良天皇]]陵 *** [[白河天皇]]陵 *** [[淳和天皇]]陵 *** [[文徳天皇]]陵 *** [[光孝天皇]]陵 *** [[宇多天皇]]陵 *** [[後朱雀天皇]]陵 *** [[後冷泉天皇]]陵 *** [[村上天皇]]陵 *** [[円融天皇|圓融天皇]]陵 *** [[後村上天皇]]陵 *** [[一条天皇|一條天皇]]陵 *** [[堀河天皇]]陵 *** [[嵯峨天皇]]陵 *** [[亀山天皇|龜山天皇]]陵 *** [[後亀山天皇|後龜山天皇]]陵 *** [[清和天皇]]陵 *** [[後三条天皇|後三條天皇]]陵 *** [[二条天皇|二條天皇]]陵 *** [[花山天皇|華山天皇]]陵 *** [[三条天皇|三條天皇]]陵 *** [[後花園天皇]]陵 *** [[陽成天皇]]陵 *** [[後一条天皇|後一條天皇]]陵 *** [[冷泉天皇]]陵 *** [[高倉天皇]]陵 *** [[六條天皇]]陵 *** [[後白河天皇]]陵 *** [[後堀河天皇]]陵 *** [[四条天皇|四條天皇]]陵 *** [[後円融天皇|後圓融天皇]]陵 *** [[後小松天皇]]陵 *** [[称光天皇|稱光天皇]]陵 *** [[後土御門天皇]]陵 *** [[正親町天皇]]陵 *** [[後陽成天皇]]以降の陵

『'''山陵志'''』（さんりょうし）は、[[江戸時代]]後期に[[蒲生君平]]が著した[[書物]]で、[[天皇陵]]（山陵）に関する[[研究]][[調査]]結果を記した[[史料]]である。
== 概要 == [[蒲生君平]]が[[寛政]]8年（[[1796年]]）から寛政12年（[[1800年]]）にかけて自ら行った[[近畿地方]]や[[四国地方]]の陵墓調査結果を[[編纂]]したもので、草稿は寛政9年（[[1797年]]）に、最終稿は[[享和]]元年（[[1801年]]）に完成したと言われる。書物として発刊されたのは[[文化 (元号)|文化]]5年（[[1808年]]）とも[[文政]]5年（[[1822年]]）とも言われる。文政5年説の場合、君平の死後にはじめて出版されたことになる。「[[前方後円墳]]」という言葉は『山陵志』の中で初めて君平が用いた言葉である。本書が[[幕末]]の[[尊王論]]の根拠となったと評される一方で、調査結果を記録した[[考古学]]資料としての評価も高く、[[明治維新]]の後、[[明治天皇]]は君平の業績を讃え、[[勅|勅命]]により郷里である[[宇都宮市|宇都宮]]に[[石碑]]（蒲生君平勅旌碑）が建立された。本書は[[水戸光圀]]が編纂に取り組んだ『[[大日本史]]』の補完資料の位置付けとしても評価されている。
== 構成 == 全2巻。 * 第1巻 ** [[大和国]]の山陵（31箇所） *** [[神武天皇]]陵 *** [[綏靖天皇]]陵 *** [[安寧天皇]]陵 *** [[懿德天皇]]陵 *** [[宣化天皇]]陵 *** [[孝元天皇]]陵 *** [[天武天皇]]および[[持統天皇]]陵（合葬） *** [[欽明天皇]]陵 *** [[文武天皇]]陵 *** [[皇極天皇|齊明天皇]]陵 *** [[孝昭天皇]]陵 *** [[孝安天皇]]陵 *** [[後醍醐天皇]]陵 *** [[崇峻天皇]]陵 *** [[舒明天皇]]陵 *** [[崇神天皇]]陵 *** [[景行天皇]]陵 *** [[光仁天皇]]陵 *** [[開化天皇]]陵 *** [[聖武天皇]]陵 *** [[元明天皇]]陵 *** [[元正天皇]]陵 *** [[平城天皇]]陵 *** [[垂仁天皇]]陵 *** [[安康天皇]]陵 *** [[成務天皇]]陵 *** [[孝謙天皇]]陵 *** [[孝霊天皇|孝靈天皇]]陵 *** [[顕宗天皇|顯宗天皇]]陵 *** [[武烈天皇]]陵 ** [[河内国]]の山陵（13箇所） *** [[仲哀天皇]]陵 *** [[允恭天皇]]陵 *** [[応神天皇|應神天皇]]陵 *** [[雄略天皇]]陵 *** [[仁賢天皇]]陵 *** [[清寧天皇]]陵 *** [[安閑天皇]]陵 *** [[敏達天皇]]陵 *** [[用明天皇]]陵 *** [[推古天皇]]陵 *** [[孝徳天皇]]陵 *** [[後村上天皇]]陵 ** [[和泉国]]の山陵（3箇所） *** [[仁徳天皇]]陵 *** [[履中天皇]]陵 *** [[反正天皇]]陵 ** [[摂津国]]、[[丹波国]]、[[阿波国]]、[[淡路国]]、[[讃岐国]]、[[隠岐国]]、[[佐渡国]]（各1箇所、計7箇所） *** [[継体天皇|繼體天皇]]陵（摂津国） *** [[光厳天皇|光嚴天皇]]陵（丹波国） *** [[土御門天皇]]陵（阿波国） *** [[淳仁天皇]]陵（淡路国）- [[廃帝]]。[[孝謙天皇|孝謙上皇]]により[[皇位]]を剥奪・降格され、[[流罪|配流]]先の淡路国で没した。 *** [[崇徳天皇]]陵（讃岐国） *** [[後鳥羽天皇]]陵（隠岐国） *** [[順徳天皇]]陵（佐渡国） * 第2巻 ** [[山城国]]（30箇所） *** [[天智天皇]]陵 *** [[醍醐天皇]]陵 *** [[朱雀天皇]]陵 *** [[桓武天皇]]陵 *** [[仁明天皇]]陵 *** [[後深草天皇]]陵 *** [[伏見天皇]]陵 *** [[後伏見天皇]]陵 *** [[後柏原天皇]]陵 *** [[後奈良天皇]]陵 *** [[白河天皇]]陵 *** [[淳和天皇]]陵 *** [[文徳天皇]]陵 *** [[光孝天皇]]陵 *** [[宇多天皇]]陵 *** [[後朱雀天皇]]陵 *** [[後冷泉天皇]]陵 *** [[村上天皇]]陵 *** [[円融天皇|圓融天皇]]陵 *** [[後村上天皇]]陵 *** [[一条天皇|一條天皇]]陵 *** [[堀河天皇]]陵 *** [[嵯峨天皇]]陵 *** [[亀山天皇|龜山天皇]]陵 *** [[後亀山天皇|後龜山天皇]]陵 *** [[清和天皇]]陵 *** [[後三条天皇|後三條天皇]]陵 *** [[二条天皇|二條天皇]]陵 *** [[花山天皇|華山天皇]]陵 *** [[三条天皇|三條天皇]]陵 *** [[後花園天皇]]陵 *** [[陽成天皇]]陵 *** [[後一条天皇|後一條天皇]]陵 *** [[冷泉天皇]]陵 *** [[高倉天皇]]陵 *** [[六条天皇|六條天皇]]陵 *** [[後白河天皇]]陵 *** [[後堀河天皇]]陵 *** [[四条天皇|四條天皇]]陵 *** [[後円融天皇|後圓融天皇]]陵 *** [[後小松天皇]]陵 *** [[称光天皇|稱光天皇]]陵 *** [[後土御門天皇]]陵 *** [[正親町天皇]]陵 *** [[後陽成天皇]]以降の陵

『'''山陵志'''』（さんりょうし）は、[[江戸時代]]後期に[[蒲生君平]]が著した[[書物]]で、[[天皇陵]]（山陵）に関する[[研究]][[調査]]結果を記した[[史料]]である。 特に、この書物の中では「宮車説」を記している。
== 概要 == [[蒲生君平]]が[[寛政]]8年（[[1796年]]）から寛政12年（[[1800年]]）にかけて自ら行った[[近畿地方]]や[[四国地方]]の陵墓調査結果を[[編纂]]したもので、草稿は寛政9年（[[1797年]]）に、最終稿は[[享和]]元年（[[1801年]]）に完成したと言われる。書物として発刊されたのは[[文化 (元号)|文化]]5年（[[1808年]]）とも[[文政]]5年（[[1822年]]）とも言われる。文政5年説の場合、君平の死後にはじめて出版されたことになる。「[[前方後円墳]]」という言葉は『山陵志』の中で初めて君平が用いた言葉である。本書が[[幕末]]の[[尊王論]]の根拠となったと評される一方で、調査結果を記録した[[考古学]]資料としての評価も高く、[[明治維新]]の後、[[明治天皇]]は君平の業績を讃え、[[勅|勅命]]により郷里である[[宇都宮市|宇都宮]]に[[石碑]]（蒲生君平勅旌碑）が建立された。本書は[[水戸光圀]]が編纂に取り組んだ『[[大日本史]]』の補完資料の位置付けとしても評価されている。

『'''山陵志'''』（さんりょうし）は、[[江戸時代]]後期に[[蒲生君平]]が著した[[書物]]で、[[天皇陵]]（山陵）に関する[[研究]][[調査]]結果を記した[[史料]]である。 特に、この書物の中では「宮車説」を記している。
== 概要 == [[蒲生君平]]が[[寛政]]8年（[[1796年]]）から寛政12年（[[1800年]]）にかけて自ら行った[[近畿地方]]や[[四国地方]]の陵墓調査結果を[[編纂]]したもので、草稿は寛政9年（[[1797年]]）に、最終稿は[[享和]]元年（[[1801年]]）に完成したと言われる。書物として発刊されたのは[[文化 (元号)|文化]]5年（[[1808年]]）とも[[文政]]5年（[[1822年]]）とも言われる。文政5年説の場合、君平の死後にはじめて出版されたことになる。「[[前方後円墳]]」という言葉は『山陵志』の中で初めて君平が用いた言葉である。本書が[[幕末]]の[[尊王論]]の根拠となったと評される一方で、調査結果を記録した[[考古学]]資料としての評価も高く、[[明治維新]]の後、[[明治天皇]]は君平の業績を讃え、[[勅|勅命]]により郷里である[[宇都宮市|宇都宮]]に[[石碑]]（蒲生君平勅旌碑）が建立された。本書は[[徳川光圀]]が編纂に取り組んだ『[[大日本史]]』の補完資料の位置付けとしても評価されている。
== 構成 == 全2巻。 * 第1巻 ** [[大和国]]の山陵（31箇所） *** [[神武天皇]]陵 *** [[綏靖天皇]]陵 *** [[安寧天皇]]陵 *** [[懿徳天皇|懿德天皇]]陵 *** [[宣化天皇]]陵 *** [[孝元天皇]]陵 *** [[天武天皇]]および[[持統天皇]]陵（合葬） *** [[欽明天皇]]陵 *** [[文武天皇]]陵 *** [[皇極天皇|齊明天皇]]陵 *** [[孝昭天皇]]陵 *** [[孝安天皇]]陵 *** [[後醍醐天皇]]陵 *** [[崇峻天皇]]陵 *** [[舒明天皇]]陵 *** [[崇神天皇]]陵 *** [[景行天皇]]陵 *** [[光仁天皇]]陵 *** [[開化天皇]]陵 *** [[聖武天皇]]陵 *** [[元明天皇]]陵 *** [[元正天皇]]陵 *** [[平城天皇]]陵 *** [[垂仁天皇]]陵 *** [[安康天皇]]陵 *** [[成務天皇]]陵 *** [[孝謙天皇]]陵 *** [[孝霊天皇|孝靈天皇]]陵 *** [[顕宗天皇|顯宗天皇]]陵 *** [[武烈天皇]]陵 ** [[河内国]]の山陵（13箇所） *** [[仲哀天皇]]陵 *** [[允恭天皇]]陵 *** [[応神天皇|應神天皇]]陵 *** [[雄略天皇]]陵 *** [[仁賢天皇]]陵 *** [[清寧天皇]]陵 *** [[安閑天皇]]陵 *** [[敏達天皇]]陵 *** [[用明天皇]]陵 *** [[推古天皇]]陵 *** [[孝徳天皇]]陵 *** [[後村上天皇]]陵 ** [[和泉国]]の山陵（3箇所） *** [[仁徳天皇]]陵 *** [[履中天皇]]陵 *** [[反正天皇]]陵 ** [[摂津国]]、[[丹波国]]、[[阿波国]]、[[淡路国]]、[[讃岐国]]、[[隠岐国]]、[[佐渡国]]（各1箇所、計7箇所） *** [[継体天皇|繼體天皇]]陵（摂津国） *** [[光厳天皇|光嚴天皇]]陵（丹波国） *** [[土御門天皇]]陵（阿波国） *** [[淳仁天皇]]陵（淡路国）- [[廃帝]]。[[孝謙天皇|孝謙上皇]]により[[皇位]]を剥奪・降格され、[[流罪|配流]]先の淡路国で没した。 *** [[崇徳天皇]]陵（讃岐国） *** [[後鳥羽天皇]]陵（隠岐国） *** [[順徳天皇]]陵（佐渡国） * 第2巻 ** [[山城国]]（30箇所） *** [[天智天皇]]陵 *** [[醍醐天皇]]陵 *** [[朱雀天皇]]陵 *** [[桓武天皇]]陵 *** [[仁明天皇]]陵 *** [[後深草天皇]]陵 *** [[伏見天皇]]陵 *** [[後伏見天皇]]陵 *** [[後柏原天皇]]陵 *** [[後奈良天皇]]陵 *** [[白河天皇]]陵 *** [[淳和天皇]]陵 *** [[文徳天皇]]陵 *** [[光孝天皇]]陵 *** [[宇多天皇]]陵 *** [[後朱雀天皇]]陵 *** [[後冷泉天皇]]陵 *** [[村上天皇]]陵 *** [[円融天皇|圓融天皇]]陵 *** [[後村上天皇]]陵 *** [[一条天皇|一條天皇]]陵 *** [[堀河天皇]]陵 *** [[嵯峨天皇]]陵 *** [[亀山天皇|龜山天皇]]陵 *** [[後亀山天皇|後龜山天皇]]陵 *** [[清和天皇]]陵 *** [[後三条天皇|後三條天皇]]陵 *** [[二条天皇|二條天皇]]陵 *** [[花山天皇|華山天皇]]陵 *** [[三条天皇|三條天皇]]陵 *** [[後花園天皇]]陵 *** [[陽成天皇]]陵 *** [[後一条天皇|後一條天皇]]陵 *** [[冷泉天皇]]陵 *** [[高倉天皇]]陵 *** [[六条天皇|六條天皇]]陵 *** [[後白河天皇]]陵 *** [[後堀河天皇]]陵 *** [[四条天皇|四條天皇]]陵 *** [[後円融天皇|後圓融天皇]]陵 *** [[後小松天皇]]陵 *** [[称光天皇|稱光天皇]]陵 *** [[後土御門天皇]]陵 *** [[正親町天皇]]陵 *** [[後陽成天皇]]以降の陵

revision.text:
『'''山陵志'''』（さんりょうし）は、[[江戸時代]]後期に[[蒲生君平]]が著した[[書物]]で、[[天皇陵]]（山陵）に関する[[研究]][[調査]]結果を記した[[史料]]である。 特に、この書物の中では「宮車説」を記している。
== 概要 == [[蒲生君平]]が[[寛政]]8年（[[1796年]]）から寛政12年（[[1800年]]）にかけて自ら行った[[近畿地方]]や[[四国地方]]の陵墓調査結果を[[編纂]]したもので、草稿は寛政9年（[[1797年]]）に、最終稿は[[享和]]元年（[[1801年]]）に完成したと言われる。書物として発刊されたのは[[文化 (元号)|文化]]5年（[[1808年]]）とも[[文政]]5年（[[1822年]]）とも言われる。文政5年説の場合、君平の死後にはじめて出版されたことになる。「[[前方後円墳]]」という言葉は『山陵志』の中で初めて君平が用いた言葉である。本書が[[幕末]]の[[尊王論]]の根拠となったと評される一方で、調査結果を記録した[[考古学]]資料としての評価も高く、[[明治維新]]の後、[[明治天皇]]は君平の業績を讃え、[[勅|勅命]]により郷里である[[宇都宮市|宇都宮]]に[[石碑]]（蒲生君平勅旌碑）が建立された。本書は[[徳川光圀]]が編纂に取り組んだ『[[大日本史]]』の補完資料の位置付けとしても評価されている。
== 構成 == 全2巻。 * 第1巻 ** [[大和国]]の山陵（31箇所） *** [[神武天皇]]陵 *** [[綏靖天皇]]陵 *** [[安寧天皇]]陵 *** [[懿徳天皇|懿德天皇]]陵 *** [[宣化天皇]]陵 *** [[孝元天皇]]陵 *** [[天武天皇]]および[[持統天皇]]陵（合葬） *** [[欽明天皇]]陵 *** [[文武天皇]]陵 *** [[斉明天皇|齊明天皇]]陵 *** [[孝昭天皇]]陵 *** [[孝安天皇]]陵 *** [[後醍醐天皇]]陵 *** [[崇峻天皇]]陵 *** [[舒明天皇]]陵 *** [[崇神天皇]]陵 *** [[景行天皇]]陵 *** [[光仁天皇]]陵 *** [[開化天皇]]陵 *** [[聖武天皇]]陵 *** [[元明天皇]]陵 *** [[元正天皇]]陵 *** [[平城天皇]]陵 *** [[垂仁天皇]]陵 *** [[安康天皇]]陵 *** [[成務天皇]]陵 *** [[孝謙天皇]]陵 *** [[孝霊天皇|孝靈天皇]]陵 *** [[顕宗天皇|顯宗天皇]]陵 *** [[武烈天皇]]陵 ** [[河内国]]の山陵（13箇所） *** [[仲哀天皇]]陵 *** [[允恭天皇]]陵 *** [[応神天皇|應神天皇]]陵 *** [[雄略天皇]]陵 *** [[仁賢天皇]]陵 *** [[清寧天皇]]陵 *** [[安閑天皇]]陵 *** [[敏達天皇]]陵 *** [[用明天皇]]陵 *** [[推古天皇]]陵 *** [[孝徳天皇]]陵 *** [[後村上天皇]]陵 ** [[和泉国]]の山陵（3箇所） *** [[仁徳天皇]]陵 *** [[履中天皇]]陵 *** [[反正天皇]]陵 ** [[摂津国]]、[[丹波国]]、[[阿波国]]、[[淡路国]]、[[讃岐国]]、[[隠岐国]]、[[佐渡国]]（各1箇所、計7箇所） *** [[継体天皇|繼體天皇]]陵（摂津国） *** [[光厳天皇|光嚴天皇]]陵（丹波国） *** [[土御門天皇]]陵（阿波国） *** [[淳仁天皇]]陵（淡路国）- [[廃帝]]。[[孝謙天皇|孝謙上皇]]により[[皇位]]を剥奪・降格され、[[流罪|配流]]先の淡路国で没した。 *** [[崇徳天皇]]陵（讃岐国） *** [[後鳥羽天皇]]陵（隠岐国） *** [[順徳天皇]]陵（佐渡国） * 第2巻 ** [[山城国]]（30箇所） *** [[天智天皇]]陵 *** [[醍醐天皇]]陵 *** [[朱雀天皇]]陵 *** [[桓武天皇]]陵 *** [[仁明天皇]]陵 *** [[後深草天皇]]陵 *** [[伏見天皇]]陵 *** [[後伏見天皇]]陵 *** [[後柏原天皇]]陵 *** [[後奈良天皇]]陵 *** [[白河天皇]]陵 *** [[淳和天皇]]陵 *** [[文徳天皇]]陵 *** [[光孝天皇]]陵 *** [[宇多天皇]]陵 *** [[後朱雀天皇]]陵 *** [[後冷泉天皇]]陵 *** [[村上天皇]]陵 *** [[円融天皇|圓融天皇]]陵 *** [[後村上天皇]]陵 *** [[一条天皇|一條天皇]]陵 *** [[堀河天皇]]陵 *** [[嵯峨天皇]]陵 *** [[亀山天皇|龜山天皇]]陵 *** [[後亀山天皇|後龜山天皇]]陵 *** [[清和天皇]]陵 *** [[後三条天皇|後三條天皇]]陵 *** [[二条天皇|二條天皇]]陵 *** [[花山天皇|華山天皇]]陵 *** [[三条天皇|三條天皇]]陵 *** [[後花園天皇]]陵 *** [[陽成天皇]]陵 *** [[後一条天皇|後一條天皇]]陵 *** [[冷泉天皇]]陵 *** [[高倉天皇]]陵 *** [[六条天皇|六條天皇]]陵 *** [[後白河天皇]]陵 *** [[後堀河天皇]]陵 *** [[四条天皇|四條天皇]]陵 *** [[後円融天皇|後圓融天皇]]陵 *** [[後小松天皇]]陵 *** [[称光天皇|稱光天皇]]陵 *** [[後土御門天皇]]陵 *** [[正親町天皇]]陵 *** [[後陽成天皇]]以降の陵
== 外部リンク == * {{国立国会図書館のデジタル化資料|2538092|山陵志 2巻}} * {{国立国会図書館のデジタル化資料|2538879|山陵志 2巻}} * [http://www.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kotenseki/search.php?cndbn=%8ER%97%CB%8Eu 山陵志]、[[早稲田大学]]古典籍総合データベース * [http://www.tamagawa.ac.jp/museum/archive/2002/128_1.html 蒲生君平「山陵志」]、[[玉川大学]]教育博物館
{{DEFAULTSORT:さんりようし}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:江戸時代の歴史書]] [[Category:栃木県の歴史]] [[Category:日本漢文の典籍]]

Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages). == Overview == {{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in..."
revision.text:
'''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by {{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} during the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, documenting the results of research and investigations on {{ill|Imperial mausolea of Japan|ja|天皇陵}} (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
{{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the {{ill|Kinki region|ja|近畿地方}} and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu period|ja|幕末}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a supplemental resource to the {{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}}.
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.
* Volume 1: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (31 sites) *** {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Annei|ja|安寧天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Itoku|ja|懿徳天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}} Mausoleum *** Combined mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} *** {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|ja|後醍醐天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Sushun|ja|崇峻天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Heizei|ja|平城天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ankō|ja|安康天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} Mausoleum ** Mausolea in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} (13 sites) *** {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Seinei|ja|清寧天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Go-Murakami|ja|後村上天皇}} Mausoleum ** Mausolea in {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}} (3 sites) *** {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}} Mausoleum
* Volume 2: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}} (30 sites)
== External Links == * {{NDL Digital Materials|2538092|Sanryōshi Volume 2}} * {{NDL Digital Materials|2538879|Sanryōshi Volume 2}} * [https://www.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kotenseki/search.php?cndbn=%8ER%97%CB%8Eu Sanryōshi], {{ill|Waseda University|ja|早稲田大学}} Classical Text Database * [http://www.tamagawa.ac.jp/museum/archive/2002/128_1.html Kunpei Gamo’s "Sanryōshi"], {{ill|Tamagawa University|ja|玉川大学}} Education Museum
[[Category:Edo period historical texts]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese classical Chinese texts]] [[ja:山陵志]]

'''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by {{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} during the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, documenting the results of research and investigations on {{ill|Imperial mausolea of Japan|ja|天皇陵}} (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).

[[Category:Edo period historical texts]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese classical Chinese texts]] [[Category:New pages]][[Category:Pages_with_redlinks_to_fill]][[ja:山陵志]]

'''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by {{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} during the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, documenting the results of research and investigations on {{ill|Imperial mausolea of Japan|ja|天皇陵}} (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
{{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the {{ill|Kinki region|ja|近畿地方}} and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu period|ja|幕末}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a supplemental resource to the {{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}}.
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.
* Volume 1: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (31 sites) *** {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Annei|ja|安寧天皇}} [[Category:Edo period historical texts]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese classical Chinese texts]] [[Category:New pages]][[Category:Pages_with_redlinks_to_fill]][[Category:New pages]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Sanjin-shi]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Sanjin-shi]][[Category:pp]] [[ja:山陵志]]

'''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by {{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} during the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, documenting the results of research and investigations on {{ill|Imperial mausolea of Japan|ja|天皇陵}} (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
{{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the {{ill|Kinki region|ja|近畿地方}} and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu period|ja|幕末}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a supplemental resource to the {{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}}.
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.

[[Category:Edo period historical texts]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese classical Chinese texts]] [[Category:Pages_with_redlinks_to_fill]][[Category:pp]][[category:Sanjin-shi]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Sanjin-shi]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Sanjin-shi]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Sanjin-shi]][[Category:pp]] [[ja:山陵志]]

'''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by {{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} during the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, documenting the results of research and investigations on {{ill|Imperial mausolea of Japan|ja|天皇陵}} (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
{{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the {{ill|Kinki region|ja|近畿地方}} and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu period|ja|幕末}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a supplemental resource to the {{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}}.
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.

and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} *** {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}} '''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by {{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} during the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, documenting the results of research and investigations on {{ill|Imperial mausolea of Japan|ja|天皇陵}} (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
{{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the {{ill|Kinki region|ja|近畿地方}} and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu period|ja|幕末}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}. '''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by {{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} during the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, documenting the results of research and investigations on {{ill|Imperial mausolea of Japan|ja|天皇陵}} (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
{{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the {{ill|Kinki region|ja|近畿地方}} and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu period|ja|幕末}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a supplemental resource to the {{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}}.
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.
* Volume 1: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (31 sites) *** {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Annei|ja|安寧天皇}} '''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by {{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} during the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, documenting the results of research and investigations on {{ill|Imperial mausolea of Japan|ja|天皇陵}} (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
{{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the {{ill|Kinki region|ja|近畿地方}} and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu period|ja|幕末}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a supplemental resource to the {{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}}.
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.
* Volume 1: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (31 sites) *** {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Annei|ja|安寧天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} *** {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ankō|ja|安康天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} Mausoleum *** The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu period|ja|幕末}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a supplemental resource to the {{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}}.

'''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by {{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} during the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, documenting the results of research and investigations on {{ill|Imperial mausolea of Japan|ja|天皇陵}} (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
{{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the {{ill|Kinki region|ja|近畿地方}} and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu period|ja|幕末}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a supplemental resource to the {{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}}.

'''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by {{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} during the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, documenting the results of research and investigations on {{ill|Imperial mausolea of Japan|ja|天皇陵}} (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
{{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the {{ill|Kinki region|ja|近畿地方}} and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu period|ja|幕末}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a supplemental resource to the {{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}}.
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.
* Volume 1: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (31 sites) *** {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Annei|ja|安寧天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Itoku|ja|懿徳天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}} Mausoleum *** Combined mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} *** {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ankō|ja|安康天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} Mausoleum ** Mausolea in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} (13 sites) *** {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} Mausoleum *** revision.text:
'''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by {{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} during the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, documenting the results of research and investigations on {{ill|Imperial mausolea of Japan|ja|天皇陵}} (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
{{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the {{ill|Kinki region|ja|近畿地方}} and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu period|ja|幕末}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a supplemental resource to the {{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}}.
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.
* Volume 1: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (31 sites) *** {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Annei|ja|安寧天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} *** {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|ja|後醍醐天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Sushun|ja|崇峻天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} Mausoleum *** Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Heizei|ja|平城天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ankō|ja|安康天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} Mausoleum ** Mausolea in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} (13 sites) *** {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor '''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by {{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} during the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, documenting the results of research and investigations on {{ill|Imperial mausolea of Japan|ja|天皇陵}} (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
{{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the {{ill|Kinki region|ja|近畿地方}} and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu period|ja|幕末}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a supplemental resource to the {{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}}.
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.
* Volume 1: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (31 sites) *** {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Annei|ja|安寧天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} *** {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}} Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Heizei|ja|平城天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ankō|ja|安康天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} Mausoleum ** Mausolea in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} (13 sites) *** {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Seinei|ja|清寧天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Go-Murakami|ja|後村上天皇}} Mausoleum ** Mausolea in {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}} (3 sites) *** {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}} Mausoleum

== Overview ==
{{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the {{ill|Kinki region|ja|近畿地方}} and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu period|ja|幕末}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a supplemental resource to the {{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}}.
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.
* Volume 1: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (31 sites) *** {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Annei|ja|安寧天皇}} '''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by {{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} during the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, documenting the results of research and investigations on {{ill|Imperial mausolea of Japan|ja|天皇陵}} (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
{{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the {{ill|Kinki region|ja|近畿地方}} and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu period|ja|幕末}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a supplemental resource to the {{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}}.
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.

'''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by {{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} during the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, documenting the results of research and investigations on {{ill|Imperial mausolea of Japan|ja|天皇陵}} (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
{{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the {{ill|Kinki region|ja|近畿地方}} and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu period|ja|幕末}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a supplemental resource to the {{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}}.
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.
* Volume 1: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (31 sites) *** {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Annei|ja|安寧天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Itoku|ja|懿徳天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}} Mausoleum *** Combined mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} *** {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|ja|後醍醐天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Sushun|ja|崇峻天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} Mausoleum *** Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ankō|ja|安康天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} Mausoleum ** Mausolea in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} (13 sites) *** {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by {{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} during the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, documenting the results of research and investigations on {{ill|Imperial mausolea of Japan|ja|天皇陵}} (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
{{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the {{ill|Kinki region|ja|近畿地方}} and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu period|ja|幕末}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a supplemental resource to the {{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}}.
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.
* Volume 1: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (31 sites) *** {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Annei|ja|安寧天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Itoku|ja|懿徳天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}} Mausoleum *** Combined mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} *** {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|ja|後醍醐天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Sushun|ja|崇峻天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Heizei|ja|平城天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ankō|ja|安康天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} Mausoleum ** Mausolea in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} (13 sites) *** {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Seinei|ja|清寧天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Go-Murakami|ja|後村上天皇}} Mausoleum ** Mausolea in {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}} (3 sites) *** {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}} Mausoleum
* Volume 2: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}} (30 sites)

revision.text:
'''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by {{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} during the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, documenting the results of research and investigations on {{ill|Imperial mausolea of Japan|ja|天皇陵}} (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
{{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the {{ill|Kinki region|ja|近畿地方}} and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu period|ja|幕末}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a supplemental resource to the {{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}}.
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.
* Volume 1: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (31 sites) *** {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Annei|ja|安寧天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Itoku|ja|懿徳天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}} Mausoleum *** Combined mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} *** {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|ja|後醍醐天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Sushun|ja|崇峻天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Heizei|ja|平城天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ankō|ja|安康天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} Mausoleum ** Mausolea in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} (13 sites) *** {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Seinei|ja|清寧天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Go-Murakami|ja|後村上天皇}} Mausoleum ** Mausolea in {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}} (3 sites) *** {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}} Mausoleum
* Volume 2: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}} (30 sites)

'''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by {{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} during the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, documenting the results of research and investigations on {{ill|Imperial mausolea of Japan|ja|天皇陵}} (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
{{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the {{ill|Kinki region|ja|近畿地方}} and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu period|ja|幕末}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a supplemental resource to the {{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}}.
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.
* Volume 1: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (31 sites) *** {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Annei|ja|安寧天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Itoku|ja|懿徳天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}} Mausoleum *** Combined mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} *** {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|ja|後醍醐天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Sushun|ja|崇峻天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} Mausoleum *** revision.text:
'''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by {{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} during the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, documenting the results of research and investigations on {{ill|Imperial mausolea of Japan|ja|天皇陵}} (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
{{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the {{ill|Kinki region|ja|近畿地方}} and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu period|ja|幕末}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a supplemental resource to the {{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}}.
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.
* Volume 1: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (31 sites) *** {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Annei|ja|安寧天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Itoku|ja|懿徳天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}} Mausoleum *** Combined mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} *** {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|ja|後醍醐天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Sushun|ja|崇峻天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Heizei|ja|平城天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ankō|ja|安康天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} Mausoleum ** Mausolea in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} (13 sites) *** {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Seinei|ja|清寧天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Go-Murakami|ja|後村上天皇}} Mausoleum ** Mausolea in {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}} (3 sites) *** {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}} Mausoleum
* Volume 2: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}} (30 sites)

'''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by {{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} during the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, documenting the results of research and investigations on {{ill|Imperial mausolea of Japan|ja|天皇陵}} (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
{{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the {{ill|Kinki region|ja|近畿地方}} and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu period|ja|幕末}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a supplemental resource to the {{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}}.
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.
* Volume 1: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (31 sites) *** {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Annei|ja|安寧天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Itoku|ja|懿徳天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}} Mausoleum *** Combined mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} *** {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|ja|後醍醐天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Sushun|ja|崇峻天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} Mausoleum *** Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ankō|ja|安康天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} Mausoleum ** Mausolea in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} (13 sites) *** {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor '''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by {{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} during the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, documenting the results of research and investigations on {{ill|Imperial mausolea of Japan|ja|天皇陵}} (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
{{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the {{ill|Kinki region|ja|近畿地方}} and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu period|ja|幕末}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a suqqlemental resource to the {{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}}.
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.
* Volume 1: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (31 sites) *** {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Annei|ja|安寧天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Itoku|ja|懿徳天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}} Mausoleum *** Combined mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} *** {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|ja|後醍醐天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Sushun|ja|崇峻天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} Mausoleum ** Mausolea in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} (13 sites) *** {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} Mausoleum *** revision.comment: Text replacement - "|天皇陵}}" to "|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}}"
revision.text:
'''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by {{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} during the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, documenting the results of research and investigations on {{ill|Imperial mausolea of Japan|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}} (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
{{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the {{ill|Kinki region|ja|近畿地方}} and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu period|ja|幕末}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a suqqlemental resource to the {{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}}.
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.
* Volume 1: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (31 sites) *** {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Annei|ja|安寧天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Itoku|ja|懿徳天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}} Mausoleum *** Combined mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} *** {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|ja|後醍醐天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Sushun|ja|崇峻天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Heizei|ja|平城天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ankō|ja|安康天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} Mausoleum ** Mausolea in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} (13 sites) *** {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Seinei|ja|清寧天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Go-Murakami|ja|後村上天皇}} Mausoleum ** Mausolea in {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}} (3 sites) *** {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}} Mausoleum
* Volume 2: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}} (30 sites)

'''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by {{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} during the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, documenting the results of research and investigations on {{ill|Imperial mausolea of Japan|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}} (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
{{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the {{ill|Kinki region|ja|近畿地方}} and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu period|ja|幕末}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a suqqlemental resource to the {{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}}.
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.

A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu period|ja|幕末}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a suqqlemental resource to the {{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}}.
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.
* Volume 1: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (31 sites) *** {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Annei|ja|安寧天皇}} '''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by {{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} during the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, documenting the results of research and investigations on {{ill|Imperial mausolea of Japan|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}} (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
{{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the {{ill|Kinki region|ja|近畿地方}} and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu period|ja|幕末}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a suqqlemental resource to the {{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}}.
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.
* Volume 1: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (31 sites) *** {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Annei|ja|安寧天皇}} '''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by {{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} during the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, documenting the results of research and investigations on {{ill|Imperial mausolea of Japan|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}} (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
{{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the {{ill|Kinki region|ja|近畿地方}} and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu period|ja|幕末}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a suqqlemental resource to the {{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}}.
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.
* Volume 1: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} (31 sites) *** {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Annei|ja|安寧天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Itoku|ja|懿徳天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}} Mausoleum *** Combined mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} *** {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇}} Mausoleum *** {{ill|Emperor
