revision.comment: Text replacement - "|天武天皇}}" to "|天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu}}"
revision.text:
'''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by {{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} during the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, documenting the results of research and investigations on {{ill|Imperial mausolea of Japan|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}} (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
{{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the {{ill|Kinki region|ja|近畿地方}} and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu period|ja|幕末}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a su11lemental resource to the {{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}}.
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.
* Volume 1: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} (31 sites) **** {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Annei|ja|安寧天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Itoku|ja|懿徳天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}} Mausoleum **** Combined mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} **** {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|ja|後醍醐天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Sushun|ja|崇峻天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Heizei|ja|平城天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Ankō|ja|安康天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} Mausoleum ** Mausolea in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} (13 sites) **** {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Seinei|ja|清寧天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Go-Murakami|ja|後村上天皇}} Mausoleum ** Mausolea in {{ill|Izumi * Volume 2: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province }} (30 sites)

'''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by {{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} during the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, documenting the results of research and investigations on {{ill|Imperial mausolea of Japan|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}} (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
{{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the {{ill|Kinki region|ja|近畿地方}} and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu period|ja|幕末}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a su11lemental resource to the {{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}}.
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.
* Volume 1: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} (31 sites) **** {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Annei|ja|安寧天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Itoku|ja|懿徳天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}} Mausoleum **** Combined mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} **** {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Empress '''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by {{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} during the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, documenting the results of research and investigations on {{ill|Imperial mausolea of Japan|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}} (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
{{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平}} compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the {{ill|Kinki region|ja|近畿地方}} and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu period|ja|幕末}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a su11lemental resource to the {{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}}.
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.
* Volume 1: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} (31 sites) **** {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Annei|ja|安寧天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Itoku|ja|懿徳天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}} Mausoleum **** Combined mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} **** {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|ja|後醍醐天皇}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Sushun|ja|崇峻天皇}} revision.text:
'''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by {{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平|12=draft}} during the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, documenting the results of research and investigations on {{ill|Imperial mausolea of Japan|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs|12=draft}} (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
{{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平|12=draft}} compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the {{ill|Kinki region|ja|近畿地方|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方|12=draft}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu period|ja|幕末|12=draft}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑|12=draft}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft}}. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a su11lemental resource to the {{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史|12=draft}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀|12=draft}}.
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.
* Volume 1: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft}} (31 sites) **** {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇|12=draft}} Mausoleum Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** Combined mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft}} **** {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇|12=draft}} Mausoleum Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft}} Mausoleum Genshō|ja|元正天皇|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Heizei|ja|平城天皇|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Ankō|ja|安康天皇|12=draft}} Mausoleum Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇|12=draft}} Mausoleum ** Mausolea in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|12=draft}} (13 sites) **** {{ill|Emperor Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).

{{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平|12=draft}} compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the {{ill|Kinki region|ja|近畿地方|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方|12=draft}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu period|ja|幕末|12=draft}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑|12=draft}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft}}. {{draft categories| [[Category:Edo period historical texts]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese classical Chinese texts]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Sanjin-shi]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Sanjin-shi]] [[ja:山陵志]]

'''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by {{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平|12=draft}} during the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, documenting the results of research and investigations on {{ill|Imperial mausolea of Japan|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs|12=draft}} (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
{{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平|12=draft}} compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the {{ill|Kinki region|ja|近畿地方|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方|12=draft}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu period|ja|幕末|12=draft}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑|12=draft}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft}}. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a su11lemental resource to the {{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史|12=draft}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀|12=draft}}.
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.
* Volume 1: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft}} (31 sites) **** {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Annei|ja|安寧天皇|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Itoku|ja|懿徳天皇|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** Combined mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft}} **** {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇|12=draft}} Mausoleum '''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by {{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平|12=draft|12=draft}} during the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, documenting the results of research and investigations on {{ill|Imperial mausolea of Japan|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs|12=draft|12=draft}} (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
{{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平|12=draft|12=draft}} compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the {{ill|Kinki region|ja|近畿地方|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方|12=draft|12=draft}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論|12=draft|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu period|ja|幕末|12=draft|12=draft}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft|12=draft}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑|12=draft|12=draft}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft|12=draft}}. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a su11lemental resource to the {{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史|12=draft|12=draft}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.
* Volume 1: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft|12=draft}} (31 sites) **** {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Annei|ja|安寧天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Itoku|ja|懿徳天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** Combined mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} **** {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|ja|後醍醐天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Sushun|ja|崇峻天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Heizei|ja|平城天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Ankō|ja|安康天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} Mausoleum ** Mausolea in {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Go-Murakami|ja|後村上天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} Mausoleum ** Mausolea in {{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国|12=draft|12=draft}} (3 sites) **** {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Hanzei|ja|反正天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} Mausoleum
* Volume 2: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=draft|12=draft}} (30 sites)

revision.comment: 14 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:山陵志 up to rev 96672996
revision.text:
『'''山陵志'''』（さんりょうし）は、[[江戸時代]]後期に[[蒲生君平]]が著した[[書物]]で、[[天皇陵]]（山陵）に関する[[研究]][[調査]]結果を記した[[史料]]である。 特に、この書物の中では「宮車説」を記している。
== 概要 == [[蒲生君平]]が[[寛政]]8年（[[1796年]]）から寛政12年（[[1800年]]）にかけて自ら行った[[近畿地方]]や[[四国地方]]の陵墓調査結果を[[編纂]]したもので、草稿は寛政9年（[[1797年]]）に、最終稿は[[享和]]元年（[[1801年]]）に完成したと言われる。書物として発刊されたのは[[文化 (元号)|文化]]5年（[[1808年]]）とも[[文政]]5年（[[1822年]]）とも言われる。文政5年説の場合、君平の死後にはじめて出版されたことになる。「[[前方後円墳]]」という言葉は『山陵志』の中で初めて君平が用いた言葉である。本書が[[幕末]]の[[尊王論]]の根拠となったと評される一方で、調査結果を記録した[[考古学]]資料としての評価も高く、[[明治維新]]の後、[[明治天皇]]は君平の業績を讃え、[[勅|勅命]]により郷里である[[宇都宮市|宇都宮]]に[[石碑]]（蒲生君平勅旌碑）が建立された。本書は[[徳川光圀]]が編纂に取り組んだ『[[大日本史]]』の補完資料の位置付けとしても評価されている。
== 構成 == 全2巻。 * 第1巻 ** [[大和国]]の山陵（31箇所） *** [[神武天皇]]陵 *** [[綏靖天皇]]陵 *** [[安寧天皇]]陵 *** [[懿徳天皇|懿德天皇]]陵 *** [[宣化天皇]]陵 *** [[孝元天皇]]陵 *** [[天武天皇]]および[[持統天皇]]陵（合葬） *** [[欽明天皇]]陵 *** [[文武天皇]]陵 *** [[斉明天皇|齊明天皇]]陵 *** [[孝昭天皇]]陵 *** [[孝安天皇]]陵 *** [[後醍醐天皇]]陵 *** [[崇峻天皇]]陵 *** [[舒明天皇]]陵 *** [[崇神天皇]]陵 *** [[景行天皇]]陵 *** [[光仁天皇]]陵 *** [[開化天皇]]陵 *** [[聖武天皇]]陵 *** [[元明天皇]]陵 *** [[元正天皇]]陵 *** [[平城天皇]]陵 *** [[垂仁天皇]]陵 *** [[安康天皇]]陵 *** [[成務天皇]]陵 *** [[孝謙天皇]]陵 *** [[孝霊天皇|孝靈天皇]]陵 *** [[顕宗天皇|顯宗天皇]]陵 *** [[武烈天皇]]陵 ** [[河内国]]の山陵（13箇所） *** [[仲哀天皇]]陵 *** [[允恭天皇]]陵 *** [[応神天皇|應神天皇]]陵 *** [[雄略天皇]]陵 *** [[仁賢天皇]]陵 *** [[清寧天皇]]陵 *** [[安閑天皇]]陵 *** [[敏達天皇]]陵 *** [[用明天皇]]陵 *** [[推古天皇]]陵 *** [[孝徳天皇]]陵 *** [[後村上天皇]]陵 ** [[和泉国]]の山陵（3箇所） *** [[仁徳天皇]]陵 *** [[履中天皇]]陵 *** [[反正天皇]]陵 ** [[摂津国]]、[[丹波国]]、[[阿波国]]、[[淡路国]]、[[讃岐国]]、[[隠岐国]]、[[佐渡国]]（各1箇所、計7箇所） *** [[継体天皇|繼體天皇]]陵（摂津国） *** [[光厳天皇|光嚴天皇]]陵（丹波国） *** [[土御門天皇]]陵（阿波国） *** [[淳仁天皇]]陵（淡路国）- [[廃帝]]。[[孝謙天皇|孝謙上皇]]により[[皇位]]を剥奪・降格され、[[流罪|配流]]先の淡路国で没した。 *** [[崇徳天皇]]陵（讃岐国） *** [[後鳥羽天皇]]陵（隠岐国） *** [[順徳天皇]]陵（佐渡国） * 第2巻 ** [[山城国]]（30箇所） *** [[天智天皇]]陵 *** [[醍醐天皇]]陵 *** [[朱雀天皇]]陵 *** [[桓武天皇]]陵 *** [[仁明天皇]]陵 *** [[後深草天皇]]陵 *** [[伏見天皇]]陵 *** [[後伏見天皇]]陵 *** [[後柏原天皇]]陵 *** [[後奈良天皇]]陵 *** [[白河天皇]]陵 *** [[淳和天皇]]陵 *** [[文徳天皇]]陵 *** [[光孝天皇]]陵 *** [[宇多天皇]]陵 *** [[後朱雀天皇]]陵 *** [[後冷泉天皇]]陵 *** [[村上天皇]]陵 *** [[円融天皇|圓融天皇]]陵 *** [[後村上天皇]]陵 *** [[一条天皇|一條天皇]]陵 *** [[堀河天皇]]陵 *** [[嵯峨天皇]]陵 *** [[亀山天皇|龜山天皇]]陵 *** [[後亀山天皇|後龜山天皇]]陵 *** [[清和天皇]]陵 *** [[後三条天皇|後三條天皇]]陵 *** [[二条天皇|二條天皇]]陵 *** [[花山天皇|華山天皇]]陵 *** [[三条天皇|三條天皇]]陵 *** [[後花園天皇]]陵 *** [[陽成天皇]]陵 *** [[後一条天皇|後一條天皇]]陵 *** [[冷泉天皇]]陵 *** [[高倉天皇]]陵 *** [[六条天皇|六條天皇]]陵 *** [[後白河天皇]]陵 *** [[後堀河天皇]]陵 *** [[四条天皇|四條天皇]]陵 *** [[後円融天皇|後圓融天皇]]陵 *** [[後小松天皇]]陵 *** [[称光天皇|稱光天皇]]陵 *** [[後土御門天皇]]陵 *** [[正親町天皇]]陵 *** [[後陽成天皇]]以降の陵
== 外部リンク == * {{国立国会図書館のデジタル化資料|2538092|山陵志 2巻}} * {{国立国会図書館のデジタル化資料|2538879|山陵志 2巻}} * [https://www.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kotenseki/search.php?cndbn=%8ER%97%CB%8Eu 山陵志]、[[早稲田大学]]古典籍総合データベース * [http://www.tamagawa.ac.jp/museum/archive/2002/128_1.html 蒲生君平「山陵志」]、[[玉川大学]]教育博物館
{{DEFAULTSORT:さんりようし}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:江戸時代の歴史書]] [[Category:栃木県の歴史]] [[Category:日本漢文の典籍]]

'''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by {{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, documenting the results of research and investigations on {{ill|Imperial mausolea of Japan|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
{{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平|qq=draft|qq=draft}} compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the {{ill|Kinki region|ja|近畿地方|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方|qq=draft|qq=draft}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu period|ja|幕末|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a su11lemental resource to the {{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.
* Volume 1: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (31 sites) **** {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Annei|ja|安寧天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Itoku|ja|懿徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mausoleum **** Combined mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} **** {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|ja|後醍醐天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Sushun|ja|崇峻天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Jomei|ja|舒明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Heizei|ja|平城天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Ankō|ja|安康天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|ja|孝霊天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Kenzo|ja|顕宗天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mausoleum ** Mausolea in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (13 sites) **** {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mausoleum **** Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Richū|ja|履中天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Hanzei|ja|反正天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mausoleum
* Volume 2: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (30 sites)

After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a su11lemental resource to the {{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.

The book is composed of two volumes.

'''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by {{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, documenting the results of research and investigations on {{ill|Imperial mausolea of Japan|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
{{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平|qq=draft|qq=draft}} compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the {{ill|Kinki region|ja|近畿地方|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方|qq=draft|qq=draft}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu period|ja|幕末|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a su11lemental resource to the {{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.
* Volume 1: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (31 sites) **** {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Annei|ja|安寧天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Itoku|ja|懿徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonnō theory|qq=}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu period|ja|幕末|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bakumatsu period|qq=}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|qq=}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya|qq=}}. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a su11lemental resource to the {{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainihonshi|qq=}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Mitsukuni|qq=}}.
== Structure ==

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|前方後円墳|" to "|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|"
revision.text:
'''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by {{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunpei Gamo|qq=}} during the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, documenting the results of research and investigations on {{ill|Imperial mausolea of Japan|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial mausolea of Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese imperial tombs|qq=}} (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
{{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunpei Gamo|qq=}} compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the {{ill|Kinki region|ja|近畿地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki region|qq=}} and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikoku region|qq=}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonnō theory|qq=}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu period|ja|幕末|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bakumatsu period|qq=}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|qq=}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya|qq=}}. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a su11lemental resource to the {{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainihonshi|qq=}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Mitsukuni|qq=}}.
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.
* Volume 1: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}} (31 sites) **** {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jimmu|qq=}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Suizei|qq=}} revision.text:
'''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by {{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunpei Gamo|qq=}} during the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, documenting the results of research and investigations on {{ill|Imperial mausolea of Japan|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial mausolea of Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese imperial tombs|qq=}} (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
{{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunpei Gamo|qq=}} compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the {{ill|Kinki region|ja|近畿地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki region|qq=}} and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikoku region|qq=}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonnō theory|qq=}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu period|ja|幕末|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bakumatsu period|qq=}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|qq=}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya|qq=}}. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a su11lemental resource to the {{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainihonshi|qq=}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Mitsukuni|qq=}}.
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.
* Volume 1: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国|qq=Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}} (31 sites) **** {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jimmu|qq=}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Suizei|qq=}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Annei|ja|安寧天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Annei|qq=}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Itoku|ja|懿徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Itoku|qq=}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Senka|qq=}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kōgen|qq=}} Mausoleum **** Combined mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jitō|qq=}} **** {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kinmei|qq=}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Monmu|ja|文武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Saimei|qq=}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kōshō|qq=}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Kōan|ja|孝安天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kōan|qq=}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|ja|後醍醐天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Daigo|qq=}} revision.text:
'''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by {{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunpei Gamo|qq=}} during the late {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, documenting the results of research and investigations on {{ill|Imperial mausolea of Japan|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial mausolea of Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese imperial tombs|qq=}} (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
{{ill|Kunpei Gamo|ja|蒲生君平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunpei Gamo|qq=}} compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the {{ill|Kinki region|ja|近畿地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki region|qq=}} and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikoku region|qq=}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonnō theory|qq=}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu period|ja|幕末|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bakumatsu period|qq=}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|qq=}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya|qq=}}. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a su11lemental resource to the {{ill|Dainihonshi|ja|大日本史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainihonshi|qq=}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Mitsukuni|qq=}}.
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.
* Volume 1: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国|qq=Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}} (31 sites) **** {{ill|Emperor '''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by [[:en:Gamō Kunpei|Kunpei Gamo]] during the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], documenting the results of research and investigations on [[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]] (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
[[:en:Gamō Kunpei|Kunpei Gamo]] compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kinki region]] and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikoku region|qq=}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonnō theory|qq=}} during the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu period]] and is valued for its archaeological records. After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|qq=}}, in his hometown of [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]]. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a su11lemental resource to the [[:en:Dai Nihonshi|Dainihonshi]], compiled under [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]].
== Structure ==

'''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by [[:en:Gamō Kunpei|Kunpei Gamo]] during the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], documenting the results of research and investigations on [[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]] (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
[[:en:Gamō Kunpei|Kunpei Gamo]] compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kinki region]] and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikoku region|qq=}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonnō theory|qq=}} during the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu period]] and is valued for its archaeological records. After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|qq=}}, in his hometown of [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]]. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a su11lemental resource to the [[:en:Dai Nihonshi|Dainihonshi]], compiled under [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]].
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Japanese classical Chinese texts
revision.text:
'''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by [[:en:Gamō Kunpei|Kunpei Gamo]] during the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], documenting the results of research and investigations on [[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]] (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
[[:en:Gamō Kunpei|Kunpei Gamo]] compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kinki region]] and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikoku region|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 四国}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonnō theory|qq=}} during the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu period]] and is valued for its archaeological records. After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in his hometown of [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]]. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a su11lemental resource to the [[:en:Dai Nihonshi|Dainihonshi]], compiled under [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]].
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.
* Volume 1: ** Mausolea in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] (31 sites) **** [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Suizei|Emperor Suizei]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Annei|Emperor Annei]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Itoku|Emperor Itoku]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] Mausoleum **** Combined [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] **** [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Empress Kōgyoku|Empress Saimei]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Kōshō|Emperor Kōshō]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Kōan|Emperor Kōan]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Sushun|Emperor Sushun]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Heizei|Emperor Heizei]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Ankō|Emperor Ankō]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Kenzō|Emperor Kenzo]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]] Mausoleum ** Mausolea in [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] (13 sites) **** [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] Mausoleum **** {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11471138]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by [[:en:Gamō Kunpei|Kunpei Gamo]] during the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], documenting the results of research and investigations on [[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]] (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
[[:en:Gamō Kunpei|Kunpei Gamo]] compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kinki region]] and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikoku region|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 四国|1=Shikoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13991}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonnō theory|qq=|1=sonnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458667}} during the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu period]] and is valued for its archaeological records. After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in his hometown of [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]]. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a su11lemental resource to the [[:en:Dai Nihonshi|Dainihonshi]], compiled under [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]].
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.
* Volume 1: ** Mausolea in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] (31 sites) **** [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Suizei|Emperor Suizei]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Annei|Emperor Annei]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Itoku|Emperor Itoku]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] Mausoleum **** Combined mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] **** [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Empress Kōgyoku|Empress Saimei]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Kōshō|Emperor Kōshō]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Kōan|Emperor Kōan]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Sushun|Emperor Sushun]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Heizei|Emperor Heizei]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Ankō|Emperor Ankō]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Kenzō|Emperor Kenzo]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]] Mausoleum ** Mausolea in [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] (13 sites) **** [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Seinei|Emperor Seinei]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Go-Murakami|Emperor Go-Murakami]] Mausoleum ** Mausolea in [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] (3 sites) **** [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Richū|Emperor Richū]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Hanzei|Emperor Hanzei]] Mausoleum
* Volume 2: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (30 sites)
== External Links == * {{NDL Digital Materials|2538092|Sanryōshi Volume 2}} * {{NDL Digital Materials|2538879|Sanryōshi Volume 2}} * [https://www.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kotenseki/search.php?cndbn=%8ER%97%CB%8Eu Sanryōshi], [[:en:Waseda University|Waseda University]] Classical Text Database * [http://www.tamagawa.ac.jp/museum/archive/2002/128_1.html Kunpei Gamo’s "Sanryōshi"], [[:en:Tamagawa University|Tamagawa University]] Education Museum

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11471138]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by [[:en:Gamō Kunpei|Kunpei Gamo]] during the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], documenting the results of research and investigations on [[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]] (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
[[:en:Gamō Kunpei|Kunpei Gamo]] compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kinki region]] and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikoku region|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 四国|1=Shikoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13991}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonnō theory|qq=|1=sonnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458667}} during the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu period]] and is valued for its archaeological records. After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in his hometown of [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]]. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a su11lemental resource to the [[:en:Dai Nihonshi|Dainihonshi]], compiled under [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]].
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.
* Volume 1: ** Mausolea in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] (31 sites) **** [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Suizei|Emperor Suizei]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Annei|Emperor Annei]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Itoku|Emperor Itoku]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] Mausoleum **** Combined mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] **** [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Empress Kōgyoku|Empress Saimei]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Kōshō|Emperor Kōshō]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Kōan|Emperor Kōan]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Sushun|Emperor Sushun]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Heizei|Emperor Heizei]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Ankō|Emperor Ankō]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Kenzō|Emperor Kenzo]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]] Mausoleum ** Mausolea in [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] (13 sites) **** [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Richū|Emperor Richū]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Hanzei|Emperor Hanzei]] Mausoleum
* Volume 2: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (30 sites)

<!--Sanjin-shi--><!--Sanjin-shi--><!--Sanjin-shi--><!--Sanjin-shi--><!--Sanjin-shi--><!--Sanjin-shi--> [[Category:Kanbun]] [[Category:Edo-period history books]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11471138}}

revision.comment: Bot: Add Wikidata no short description - based on Wikidata English short description
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山陵志]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11471138]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by [[:en:Gamō Kunpei|Kunpei Gamo]] during the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], documenting the results of research and investigations on [[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]] (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
[[:en:Gamō Kunpei|Kunpei Gamo]] compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kinki region]] and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikoku region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 四国|1=Shikoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13991}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonnō theory|1=sonnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458667}} during the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu period]] and is valued for its archaeological records. After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in his hometown of [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]]. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a su11lemental resource to the [[:en:Dai Nihonshi|Dainihonshi]], compiled under [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]].
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.
* Volume 1: ** Mausolea in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] (31 sites) **** [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Suizei|Emperor Suizei]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Annei|Emperor Annei]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Itoku|Emperor Itoku]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] Mausoleum **** Combined Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] **** [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Empress Kōgyoku|Empress Saimei]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Kōshō|Emperor Kōshō]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Kōan|Emperor Kōan]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Sushun|Emperor Sushun]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Jomei|Emperor Jomei]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Heizei|Emperor Heizei]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Ankō|Emperor Ankō]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Kenzō|Emperor Kenzo]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]] Mausoleum ** Mausolea in [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] (13 sites) **** [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
[[:en:Gamō Kunpei|Kunpei Gamo]] compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kinki region]] and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikoku region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 四国|1=Shikoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13991}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonnō theory|1=sonnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458667}} during the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu period]] and is valued for its archaeological records. After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in his hometown of [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]]. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a su11lemental resource to the [[:en:Dai Nihonshi|Dainihonshi]], compiled under [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]].

<!--Sanjin-shi--><!--Sanjin-shi--><!--Sanjin-shi--><!--Sanjin-shi--><!--Sanjin-shi--><!--Sanjin-shi--> [[Category:Kanbun]] [[Category:Edo-period history books]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11471138}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山陵志]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山陵志]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11471138]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by [[:en:Gamō Kunpei|Kunpei Gamo]] during the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], documenting the results of research and investigations on [[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]] (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
[[:en:Gamō Kunpei|Kunpei Gamo]] compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kinki region]] and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikoku region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 四国|1=Shikoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13991}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonnō theory|1=sonnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458667}} during the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu period]] and is valued for its archaeological records. After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in his hometown of [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]]. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a su11lemental resource to the [[:en:Dai Nihonshi|Dainihonshi]], compiled under [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]].
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.
* Volume 1: ** Mausolea in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] (31 sites) **** [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Suizei|Emperor Suizei]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Annei|Emperor Annei]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Itoku|Emperor Itoku]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] Mausoleum **** Combined <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11471138}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Edo-period history books]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kanbun]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山陵志]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山陵志]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山陵志]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11471138]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by [[:en:Gamō Kunpei|Kunpei Gamo]] during the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], documenting the results of research and investigations on [[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]] (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
[[:en:Gamō Kunpei|Kunpei Gamo]] compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kinki region]] and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikoku region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 四国|1=Shikoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13991}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonnō theory|1=sonnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458667}} during the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu period]] and is valued for its archaeological records. After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in his hometown of [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]]. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a su11lemental resource to the [[:en:Dai Nihonshi|Dainihonshi]], compiled under [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]].
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.
* Volume 1: ** Mausolea in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] (31 sites) **** [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Suizei|Emperor Suizei]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Annei|Emperor Annei]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Itoku|Emperor Itoku]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] Mausoleum **** Combined mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] **** [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] Mausoleum **** [[:en:Emperor * Volume 2: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (30 sites)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山陵志]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山陵志]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山陵志]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11471138]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by [[:en:Gamō Kunpei|Kunpei Gamo]] during the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], documenting the results of research and investigations on [[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]] (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
[[:en:Gamō Kunpei|Kunpei Gamo]] compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kinki region]] and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikoku region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 四国|1=Shikoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13991}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonnō theory|1=sonnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458667}} during the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu period]] and is valued for its archaeological records. After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in his hometown of [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]]. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a su11lemental resource to the [[:en:Dai Nihonshi|Dainihonshi]], compiled under [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]].

Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).

{{ill|Gamō Kunpei|en|Gamō Kunpei|WD=Q6562102|lt=Kunpei Gamo}} compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kinki region}} and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikoku region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 四国|1=Shikoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13991}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonnō theory|1=sonnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458667}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu|en|Bakumatsu|WD=Q804558|lt=Bakumatsu period}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|WD=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya}}. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a su11lemental resource to the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|en|Dai Nihonshi|WD=Q1156003|lt=Dainihonshi}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|en|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|WD=Q1194944|lt=Tokugawa Mitsukuni}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山陵志]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山陵志]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山陵志]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11471138]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by {{ill|Gamō Kunpei|en|Gamō Kunpei|WD=Q6562102|lt=Kunpei Gamo}} during the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, documenting the results of research and investigations on {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|en|Japanese imperial tombs|WD=Q17218816|lt=Japanese imperial tombs}} (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
{{ill|Gamō Kunpei|en|Gamō Kunpei|WD=Q6562102|lt=Kunpei Gamo}} compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kinki region}} and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikoku region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 四国|1=Shikoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13991}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonnō theory|1=sonnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458667}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu|en|Bakumatsu|WD=Q804558|lt=Bakumatsu period}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|WD=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya}}. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a su11lemental resource to the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|en|Dai Nihonshi|WD=Q1156003|lt=Dainihonshi}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|en|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|WD=Q1194944|lt=Tokugawa Mitsukuni}}.
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.
* Volume 1: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}} (31 sites) **** {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}} Mausoleum ****
