revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山陵志]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山陵志]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山陵志]]
'''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by {{ill|Gamō Kunpei|en|Gamō Kunpei|WD=Q6562102|lt=Kunpei Gamo}} during the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, documenting the results of research and investigations on {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|en|Japanese imperial tombs|WD=Q17218816|lt=Japanese imperial tombs}} (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
{{ill|Gamō Kunpei|en|Gamō Kunpei|WD=Q6562102|lt=Kunpei Gamo}} compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kinki region}} and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikoku region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 四国|1=Shikoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13991}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonnō theory|1=sonnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458667}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu|en|Bakumatsu|WD=Q804558|lt=Bakumatsu period}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|WD=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya}}. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a su11lemental resource to the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|en|Dai Nihonshi|WD=Q1156003|lt=Dainihonshi}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|en|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|WD=Q1194944|lt=Tokugawa Mitsukuni}}.
== Structure ==
The book is composed of two volumes.
* Volume 1: ** Mausolea in {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}} (31 sites) **** {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Suizei|en|Emperor Suizei|WD=Q313321|lt=Emperor Suizei}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Annei|en|Emperor Annei|WD=Q313345|lt=Emperor Annei}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Itoku|en|Emperor Itoku|WD=Q271536|lt=Emperor Itoku}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen}} Mausoleum **** Combined mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō}} **** {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|Emperor Monmu|WD=Q357246|lt=Emperor Monmu}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|en|Empress Kōgyoku|WD=Q235296|lt=Empress Saimei}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Kōan|en|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821|lt=Emperor Kōan}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|en|Emperor Go-Daigo|WD=Q313942|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo}} Mausoleum **** {{ill|Emperor Sushun|en|Emperor Sushun|WD=Q349238|lt=Emperor Sushun}} Mausoleum **** Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a su11lemental resource to the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|en|Dai Nihonshi|WD=Q1156003|lt=Dainihonshi|1=Dai Nihonshi}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|en|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|WD=Q1194944|lt=Tokugawa Mitsukuni|1=Tokugawa Mitsukuni}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山陵志]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山陵志]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山陵志]]
'''Sanryōshi''' (山陵志) is a historical document written by {{ill|Gamō Kunpei|en|Gamō Kunpei|WD=Q6562102|lt=Kunpei Gamo|1=Gamō Kunpei}} during the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, documenting the results of research and investigations on {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|en|Japanese imperial tombs|WD=Q17218816|lt=Japanese imperial tombs|1=Japanese imperial tombs}} (sanryō). Notably, the book introduces the "kyūsha setsu" (宮車説, theory of imperial carriages).
== Overview ==
{{ill|Gamō Kunpei|en|Gamō Kunpei|WD=Q6562102|lt=Kunpei Gamo|1=Gamō Kunpei}} compiled ''Sanryōshi'' based on his surveys of mausolea in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kinki region|1=Kansai region}} and {{ill|Shikoku region|ja|四国地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikoku region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 四国|1=Shikoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13991}} conducted between Kansei 8 (1796) and Kansei 12 (1800). A draft was completed in Kansei 9 (1797), and the final version was completed in Kyōwa 1 (1801). The book was published in either Bunka 5 (1808) or Bunsei 5 (1822). If published in Bunsei 5, this would have occurred posthumously. The term {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} ("keyhole-shaped tomb mound") was first used by Kunpei in this work.
The document is praised as a source for the {{ill|Sonnō theory|ja|尊王論|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonnō theory|1=sonnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458667}} during the {{ill|Bakumatsu|en|Bakumatsu|WD=Q804558|lt=Bakumatsu period|1=Bakumatsu}} and is valued for its archaeological records. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, Emperor Meiji honored Kunpei’s achievements by commissioning a monument, {{ill|Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja|蒲生君平勅旌碑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunpei Gamo Imperial Inscription Monument|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in his hometown of {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|WD=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya|1=Utsunomiya}}. Additionally, ''Sanryōshi'' is regarded as a su11lemental resource to the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|en|Dai Nihonshi|WD=Q1156003|lt=Dainihonshi|1=Dai Nihonshi}}, compiled under {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|en|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|WD=Q1194944|lt=Tokugawa Mitsukuni|1=Tokugawa Mitsukuni}}.
== Structure ==
